question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted StreamFileData filename shorter than 10 characters to trigger a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException in `streamFileData.getFilename().substring(0, 10)`, causing the importer to crash mid-stream and leave the Hashgraph history in a partially-written, inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a filename whose first 10 characters contain path-traversal sequences (e.g., `../evil/xx`) so that `getStreamPath().resolve(streamFileData.getFilename().substring(0, 10))` resolves outside the intended streams directory, causing archived Hashgraph stream files to be written to attacker-controlled locations and corrupting the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream filename whose first 10 bytes are a valid-looking date string (e.g., `2099-01-01`) to cause the importer to archive legitimate Hashgraph stream files into a future-dated folder, making them invisible to normal date-range queries and effectively erasing them from the accessible history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] When `groupByDay` is true, can an unprivileged external user supply a filename with a null or empty value to `getFilename()` so that `substring(0, 10)` throws an exception, halting the archival pipeline and preventing further Hashgraph stream files from being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that `getNetwork()` uses `StringUtils.substringBefore(this.network, NETWORK_PREFIX_DELIMITER)` and exploit a misconfigured `network` property set to `other` (which has no default bucket in `NETWORK_DEFAULT_BUCKETS`) to cause `getBucketName()` to return an empty string, redirecting stream downloads to an attacker-controlled bucket and injecting forged Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the case-insensitive lowercasing in `getNetwork()` to craft a network value like `MAINNET` that normalizes to `mainnet`, bypassing any case-sensitive network identity checks downstream and causing the importer to pull stream files from the mainnet bucket while applying incorrect verification parameters, corrupting the Hashgraph history view?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNetworkPrefix()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getNetworkPrefix()` returns `null` when no prefix is present, causing downstream code that does not null-check the prefix to throw a NullPointerException, halting stream processing and leaving Hashgraph history in an incomplete state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNetworkPrefix()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that `getNetworkPrefix()` lowercases the prefix after the first `-` delimiter, and craft a network value like `mainnet-EVIL` to produce a prefix `evil` that is used to construct a cloud storage path, redirecting block stream downloads to an attacker-controlled folder and injecting tampered Hashgraph blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: HederaNetwork.isAllowAnonymousAccess()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `isAllowAnonymousAccess()` returns `true` only for `DEMO` to observe that if the `network` property is set to `demo`, no authentication is required to access stream files, allowing the attacker to inject unauthenticated forged stream files into the demo Hashgraph history without any credential barrier?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: HederaNetwork.hasCutover()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `hasCutover()` only returns `true` for `mainnet` and `testnet`, causing a deployment using `network=other` or `network=previewnet` to skip the cutover logic and process block streams without the cutover safety checks, allowing tampered pre-cutover Hashgraph history to be ingested unchecked?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: HederaNetwork.getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getBucketName()` returns an empty string for the `OTHER` network (no entry in `NETWORK_DEFAULT_BUCKETS`) to cause the importer to use a blank bucket name, which may resolve to a default or attacker-controlled bucket, allowing injection of forged Hashgraph record stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getBlockStreamBucketName()` returns an empty string for unknown networks to cause the block stream downloader to use an empty bucket name, potentially falling back to a default or misconfigured storage location containing attacker-injected block data that tampers with Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: nodePublicKey] Can an unprivileged external user who observes that `nodePublicKey` is a nullable, unvalidated String field exploit a deployment where this field is set to a known or weak public key, causing `AddressBookServiceImpl.getNodes()` to override all node public keys with the attacker-controlled value, bypassing signature verification and allowing forged stream files to pass as legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: nodePublicKey] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `nodePublicKey` has no `@NotNull` or format validation constraint, so if it is set to an empty string, `StringUtils.isNotBlank()` in `AddressBookServiceImpl` returns false and the override is silently skipped, while a whitespace-only value would also be skipped, creating an inconsistent key override behavior that could be exploited to selectively bypass signature verification for specific Hashgraph stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: consensusMode] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a deployment where `consensusMode` is set to `EQUAL` (all nodes equally weighted) to reduce the effective consensus threshold, making it easier for an attacker controlling a minority of nodes to reach the 1/3 stake threshold required by `ConsensusValidatorImpl`, thereby getting forged Hashgraph stream files accepted as consensus-verified history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: consensusMode] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a deployment where `consensusMode` is set to `STAKE` (not `STAKE_IN_ADDRESS_BOOK`) to include node stakes from nodes not present in the address book, inflating the total stake denominator and potentially allowing a smaller set of colluding nodes to satisfy the consensus ratio, enabling acceptance of tampered Hashgraph stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: consensusMode] Can an unprivileged external user observe that `ConsensusMode.STAKE_IN_ADDRESS_BOOK` is the default and that switching to `EQUAL` mode sets all node stakes to 1, then exploit a scenario where the address book has been tampered with to contain many attacker-controlled nodes, so that under `EQUAL` mode the attacker's nodes constitute more than 1/3 of the total, allowing forged Hashgraph stream files to reach consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: initialAddressBook] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `initialAddressBook` is a nullable `Path` with no validation constraints, so if it is set to a path pointing to an attacker-controlled file, `AddressBookServiceImpl.getInitialAddressBookFileData()` will load the attacker's address book as the bootstrap trust anchor, replacing all legitimate node public keys and allowing all subsequent forged stream files to pass signature verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: initialAddressBook] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of integrity verification on the `initialAddressBook` file path to supply a path to a malformed protobuf address book, causing `AddressBookServiceImpl.migrate()` to throw an `InvalidDatasetException` and preventing the importer from loading any valid address book, effectively halting all Hashgraph history ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: initialAddressBook] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `initialAddressBook` accepts any filesystem path without canonicalization, to supply a symlink path that resolves to a sensitive system file, causing the importer to parse it as an address book protobuf and potentially leaking file contents through error messages or corrupting the address book state used for Hashgraph stream verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: startDate] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `startDate` has no `@NotNull` constraint and defaults to null, causing `DateRangeCalculator` to set the effective start date to `Instant.EPOCH` for demo/blockstream networks, making the importer re-process all historical Hashgraph stream files from the beginning and potentially overwriting correctly-verified history with tampered data if the cloud bucket has been compromised?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: startDate] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `startDate` is accepted without upper-bound validation, so setting it to a far-future value causes `DateRangeCalculator` to throw an `InvalidConfigurationException` when `startDate > endDate`, halting the importer and preventing any new Hashgraph history from being ingested, creating a denial-of-history attack?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: endDate] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `endDate` defaults to `Utility.MAX_INSTANT_LONG` and has no upper-bound constraint, so if it is set to a past timestamp, the importer will immediately disable polling after processing files up to that date, truncating the visible Hashgraph history and making recent transactions appear non-existent?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: endDate] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between `endDate` and `Downloader.verifySignatures()` where `dataFilename.getInstant().isAfter(endDate)` disables the downloader, to set `endDate` to a timestamp just before a known fraudulent stream file, causing the importer to stop processing before reaching the file that would expose the tampering, effectively hiding manipulated Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: startBlockNumber] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Min(-1)` constraint on `startBlockNumber` (where -1 means earliest available) to set `startBlockNumber` to -1, causing the importer to re-download and re-process all block streams from the earliest available block, potentially overwriting verified Hashgraph history with tampered blocks if the upstream bucket has been modified?
